content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement user onboarding to overcome no project ideas. An advanced-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your portfolio.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis stems from lacking a core feature that provides immediate value. By building a sophisticated user onboarding flow—the 'Golden Path'—you create a high-utility module that transforms any basic CRUD app into a professional-grade SaaS experience, instantly elevating your portfolio and technical mastery.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking like a metronome counting down your wasted potential. You want to build, but every project idea feels like a toy—insignificant, derivative, and ultimately invisible to recruiters. The frustration isn't that you lack talent; it's that you lack the architectural framework to turn a simple concept into a production-ready system.

True engineering isn't just about the features; it's about the friction you remove for the user. By mastering the art of user onboarding, you move from 'coder' to 'product architect.' This is the hidden skill that separates the hobbyist from the hireable engineer. Today, we bridge that gap, turning a blank screen into a sophisticated, guided experience that showcases your ability to think about user psychology and system design.

steps.title: Architecting the State Machine
steps.description: Define the onboarding lifecycle using a finite state machine to manage user progress through registration, profile setup, and feature discovery.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust state machine schema for a multi-step user onboarding flow. Include states for 'initial_signup', 'profile_completion', 'feature_walkthrough', and 'onboarding_complete'. Use TypeScript interfaces for state transitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a state machine in src/lib/onboarding.ts based on the provided schema'

steps.title: Implementing Progressive Disclosure
steps.description: Design a UI pattern where complexity is revealed only as the user proves their engagement, reducing cognitive load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React component structure for progressive disclosure. The UI should hide advanced settings until the user completes the 'basic profile' state. Use Tailwind CSS for transitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate ProgressiveDisclosure component in src/components/onboarding'

steps.title: Integrating Contextual Tooltips
steps.description: Use an event-driven architecture to trigger tooltips based on specific user actions, ensuring the guidance feels organic rather than invasive.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design an event bus system to trigger contextual tooltips. Define events for 'first_button_click' and 'dashboard_view'. Ensure these trigger state updates in the onboarding machine.

steps.title: Building the Analytics Hook
steps.description: Capture 'drop-off' points in your onboarding flow to identify where users lose interest, a vital metric for any senior engineer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a telemetry hook that tracks user completion rates across onboarding steps. Include a function to log events to a mock API endpoint.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create useOnboardingTelemetry hook in src/hooks'

steps.title: Finalizing the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Polish the final step of the onboarding process to deliver immediate, high-value utility to the user, effectively closing the loop.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a 'Success Celebration' component that triggers when the onboarding state machine hits 'onboarding_complete'. Include a confetti effect and a redirect to the main dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement SuccessCelebration component and redirect logic'

pro_tips: Always optimize for 'Time to Value'—the faster the user reaches the primary feature, the higher your retention rate.
Use local storage to persist onboarding state, so users aren't punished for refreshing their browser mid-flow.
Implement 'Skip' buttons on every non-mandatory onboarding step; never hold a user hostage in your UI.
Treat onboarding as a modular package that can be dropped into any future project, not as hard-coded page logic.
Use A/B testing on your onboarding copy; small changes in language can increase conversion by 20% or more.
content.next_steps: Deploy your onboarding module to Vercel and create a 'case study' README that highlights the telemetry data you collected during testing.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-grade onboarding module that serves as a foundation for any SaaS you build. Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea and start refining the architecture that makes products successful. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
